model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Docs link in the navigation
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (361,205) said dx=18 dy=-243 conf=0.80 -> (379,-38)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to click the "Docs" link in the navigation bar.
The current crosshair position is shown by the red crosshair.
The target is the center of the "Docs" link.

Looking at the image:
- The "Docs" link is in the top navigation bar.
- The red crosshair is currently over the "Docs" link area, specifically inside the blue circle.

- The current image shows the crosshair at the new position (379, -38)? No, the blue circle is around the red crosshair.
- The red crosshair is at the top, center of "Docs".
- Looking at the nav bar: Home, Pricing, Docs, About, Contact.
- "Docs" seems to be around x=300.
- Let's look at the coordinate system. The image is 1000x500 (estimate).
- "Docs" is roughly at x=300, y=40.
- The red crosshair is centered on the "Docs" link.
- Let's try to be more precise. The blue circle is around the red crosshair. The crosshair is over "Docs".
- The "Docs" text center is approximately at x=295, y=43.
- The red crosshair appears to be at roughly (295, 10) if it's the very top.

- In the image, the red crosshair is inside a blue circle. This blue circle is at the top.
- The text "Docs" is below the crosshair in the black bar.
- No, the black bar is the navigation bar. "Docs" is part of it.
- The red crosshair is on the "Docs" text.
- Let's check the center of "Docs". It's approximately x=295, y=43.
- The red crosshair center is approximately (295, 10)? No, it looks like it's centered on the text.
- Looking closely at the first image, the red crosshair is at the top edge.
- The "Docs" text is slightly below the top.
- Let's look at the "Docs" text center. It's at (295, 43).
- The red crosshair is at (295, 10).
- Offset: dx = 295 - 295 = 0. dy = 43 - 10 = 33.
- Let me re-evaluate. The crosshair is the red '+' sign.
- The blue circle is centered on the crosshair.
- The red crosshair is at approximately (295, 10).
- The "Docs" text center is approximately (295, 43).
- So dy = 43 - 10 = 33.
- Wait, the crosshair is at (295, 10). The text "Docs" is at (295, 43).
- dx = 0, dy = 33.
- Let's re-read the prompt's previous iteration. It said (379, -38). If the new crosshair is at (379, -38), that would be off-screen (negative y).
- Looking at the image, the crosshair is clearly on the screen.
- The red crosshair is roughly at (295, 10).
- Let's check the "Docs" link center.

- Current crosshair is at (295, 10).
- dx = 0, dy = 33.

- The text "Docs" center is around (295, 43).
- The crosshair is at (295, 10).

- Let's check the confidence. I am fairly sure about the center of the text.
- However, is the red crosshair at (295, 10)?
- The blue circle is centered on the red crosshair.
- The red crosshair is at the top.

- Let's look at the image again. The red crosshair is at the very top.
- The nav bar height is maybe 80px.
- "Docs" is centered vertically in it. So if height is 80, center is 40.
- The crosshair is at y=10.
- So dy = 40 - 10 = 30.
- Let's refine the x. "Docs" is the third item. Home, Pricing, Docs, About, Contact.
- Total width is 1000.
- "Docs" is at roughly 30% of the way. 300.

- So dx=0, dy=30.

- The intersection is at (295, 10).
- The "Docs" text center is at (295, 43).
- dy = 43 - 10 = 33.
- Let's check if (295, 43) is the center of the "Docs" link area.
- The link area probably spans the whole column.
- I'll report dx=0, dy=33, conf=0.9.
